heroHeadline: Islamabad Talks Collapse After 21 Hours — No Deal; Trump Announces Immediate Hormuz Naval Blockade (Day 44 of War); Vance: 'They Have Chosen Not to Accept Our Terms'
heroSubtitle: The historic US-Iran talks in Islamabad, Pakistan ended without agreement on April 12, 2026 after 21 grueling hours — the longest direct diplomatic engagement between the two countries since 1979. VP Vance announced the breakdown: 'The bad news is that we have not reached an agreement — I think that's bad news for Iran much more than the United States.' Core sticking point: Iran refused an 'affirmative commitment' to abandon nuclear weapons. Within hours, Trump posted on Truth Social: 'Effective immediately, the United States Navy will begin the process of BLOCKADING any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz.' Forces are 'LOCKED AND LOADED.' The April 7 ceasefire — already fragile — is now in serious doubt. Pakistan is urging both sides to maintain the ceasefire; talks may resume remotely but no timeline exists. Trump's approval rating has fallen to 35–36% (Fox News: 59% disapproval — highest ever); Iran war: 34% approve, 66% disapprove.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model.
